business.headline: Swiggy Instamart receives 9 notices from food regulator over quality complaintsFSSAI has issued nine notices to Swiggy Instamart over consumer complaints about expired, spoiled and contaminated food deliveries. The regulator has sought detailed compliance records and warned of legal action if the response is delayed.

Apple sues OpenAI, ex-employees over alleged trade secrets theftApple has sued OpenAI and two former employees, alleging misuse of trade secrets for consumer hardware. The case sharpens tensions between partners as both companies push deeper into AI devices.

RBI auctions Rs 32,000 crore in govt bonds: Are they among the safest investments?Government bonds are back in the spotlight after the RBI's latest Rs 32,000 crore auction. What makes them different from other investments, and who are they best suited for? Have a look.

8th Pay Commission: Why its impact goes far beyond government salariesEconomists say the 8th Pay Commission will affect far more than central government salaries and pensions. Its recommendations could shape consumer demand, state finances, inflation and fiscal planning.

File Early, Stay Relaxed: Why the Income Tax Department is sending SMS alertsThousands of taxpayers are receiving SMS reminders from the Income Tax Department asking them to file their ITR early. What's behind these messages?

Sensex ends 828 points higher, Nifty above 24,200; RIL up 2%The BSE Sensex surged 827.57 points, or 1.08%, to close at 77,569.39, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 244.10 points, or 1.02%, to settle at 24,206.90.

Why wait for the IPO? Some investors are buying shares much earlier. You can tooLong before an IPO opens, and sometimes even years before a company files its draft papers with the market regulator, its shares can already be bought and sold legally in what's known as the unlisted or pre-IPO market.

Income tax department launches ITR-7 Excel utility for AY 2026-27With the utility now live, eligible taxpayers can download it, fill in the required information offline, validate the details, generate a JSON file and upload it on the Income Tax e-Filing portal to complete the return filing process.

TCS's hiring surprise: Why India's biggest IT firm is adding jobs againTCS added 9,279 employees in the April-June quarter after steep headcount declines in late 2025. The shift reflects selective hiring, redeployment and rising demand for AI and digital skills.

Govt eyes stronger forex reserves; FM likely to meet banks soonThe government's move comes at a time when India's foreign exchange reserves have declined sharply. The reserves fell from around $728 billion in February to nearly $682 billion by the end of May.

Home loan on a rented house? Here's what you can still claim under new tax regimeAs taxpayers file their ITRs, understanding home loan tax rules has become more important than ever. In this edition of 'KNOW YOUR TAX', we explain what you can still claim on a rented property under the new tax regime.

TCS, Infosys, HCLTech rise up to 4%: Why are IT stocks rising today?TCS's stronger-than-expected June-quarter results pushed IT stocks higher on Friday. The Nifty IT index climbed 1.82%, while the BSE Focused IT index gained nearly 2%.

Sensex jumps 800 points: Why is stock market rising today?Around 10 am, the BSE Sensex was trading 800.61 points, or 1.04%, higher at 77,542.43, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 247.80 points, or 1.03%, to 24,210.60.

Sensex, Nifty open in green lifted by IT stocks as TCS earnings cheer marketsThe BSE Sensex opened at 77,395.63 and climbed 676.12 points, or 0.88%, to 77,417.94 in early trade. The NSE Nifty50 rose 196.30 points, or 0.82%, to 24,159.10. The benchmarks had also ended higher in the previous session after recovering from Wednesday's sharp sell-off.

Why TCS may have to rethink its dividend to win the AI raceTCS is winning more AI work, but analysts say scaling it will need heavier investment. That could push the company to rebalance dividends and future growth spending.

EPF Scheme 2026: What it is, who benefits and what it means for youFrom simpler withdrawal rules to new safeguards for retirement savings, the EPF Scheme, 2026 brings several important changes. Here's a quick look at who stands to benefit and what the new rules mean for you.

Tax regime to e-verify: Income Tax Department's 7 golden rules for ITR filingThe Income Tax Department has issued seven golden rules for filing ITRs for AY 2026-27. The checklist urges early preparation, careful verification and timely e-verification to avoid delays and lost benefits.

TCS adds over 9,200 employees in Q1, logs biggest hiring in over a yearAlongside higher hiring, TCS reported a solid financial performance for the June quarter. Consolidated net profit stood at Rs 13,349 crore, while revenue rose 14% year-on-year to Rs 72,275 crore.

TCS Q1 results: Revenue jumps 14%; IT major declares Rs 12 interim dividendThe company posted double-digit growth in both revenue and profit, helped by steady demand from clients, especially in the banking and financial services sector. It also announced an interim dividend of Rs 12 per share for its shareholders.

Sensex, Nifty close higher despite US-Iran tensions; TCS results in focusBenchmark indices closed higher on Thursday as investors returned to beaten-down stocks after Wednesday's sharp sell-off. The rebound was led by midcaps and realty shares, while Middle East tensions, crude prices and earnings remained in focus.

entertainment.headline: Matt Damon wants to work with Shekhar Kapur after missing The Four Feathers At a Mumbai press conference for The Odyssey, actor Matt Damon said he wants to work with Shekhar Kapur. He recalled missing The Four Feathers years ago and said the wish has stayed with him.

Ramayana trailer: Ranbir Kapoor-Yash epic to get a grand launch on July 24 The makers of Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana have confirmed that the film's trailer will be unveiled on July 24. Starring Ranbir Kapoor, Yash, Sunny Deol and Sai Pallavi in key roles, the much-awaited mythological epic is set to kick off its promotional campaign with the grand launch.

Bring Matt Damon home: How The Odyssey keeps Hollywood's $600 million joke alive The old internet gag about Hollywood repeatedly rescuing actor Matt Damon finds new life with The Odyssey. This time, Damon's next great homecoming depends on nobody but himself: a quiet reminder that every great journey, on screen or off, is ultimately about finding your way back. And honestly? We'll still be cheering from the shore.

Dhamaal 4 box office Day 1: Ajay Devgn film opens big, misses Total Dhamaal record Actor Ajay Devgn's Dhamaal 4 opened strongly on July 10, beating early trade estimates on day 1. The comedy earned Rs 13.75 crore on the opening day.

Alia Bhatt stuns in purple saree, dances at bestie Akansha Ranjan's pre-wedding bash Actor Alia Bhatt grooved to best friend Akansha Ranjan Kapoor and Sharan Sharma's pre-wedding festivities in Mumbai. Several videos from the pre-wedding bash have emerged online.

Boney Kapoor expresses gratitude for PM Modi's letter blessing Anshula-Rohan Boney Kapoor shared Prime Minister Narendra Modi's congratulatory letter for Anshula Kapoor and Rohan Thakkar after their wedding. The filmmaker wrote how the letter added warmth to the family's intimate celebrations.

Vicky Donor, Badhaai Ho inspired me to do Super Subbu: Sundeep Kishan Actor Sundeep Kishan said Vicky Donor and Badhaai Ho inspired him to headline Super Subbu. He said the Telugu series uses humour and sensitivity to discuss sex education without sensationalism.

Thamizh Murugan: Dhanush rides an elephant, wields a vel in striking first look Actor Dhanush's Thamizh Murugan first look is out and marks his reunion with director Vetri Maaran for their fifth film together. The poster's powerful imagery has fuelled discussion around its cultural roots and the Lord Murugan connection.

Christopher Nolan thrilled to launch The Odyssey in India, thanks fans. Watch Filmmaker Christopher Nolan opened The Odyssey in Mumbai with actors Tom Holland and Matt Damon and thanked the audience for its response. The launch marked Nolan's first film premiere in the city, placing India among the earliest stops on the global premiere tour.

The Odyssey gets A rating, Christopher Nolan's film passes without cuts in India Filmmaker Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey reportedly received an A certificate and cleared the censors without any cuts. The approval means Indian viewers are set to watch the same version as global audiences.

Lenin X review: Akhil wins praise, but weak writing splits viewers Actor Akhil Akkineni's Lenin opened to mixed reactions on X, with viewers split over its rural action drama and Mahabharata parallels. The response highlighted his performance but questioned the writing, romance and song placement.

Aryan Khan mobbed outside Raghav Juyal's birthday party, fan falls amid chaos Aryan Khan was mobbed by fans and paparazzi while leaving Raghav Juyal's birthday party in Mumbai. The viral video drew criticism online after a fan fell amid the pushing and shoving.

The Odyssey India premiere schedule: Nolan, Tom Holland to meet fans, media, stars Christopher Nolan and Tom Holland arrived in Mumbai on Friday ahead of The Odyssey's India premiere, marking the filmmaker's first-ever official film premiere in the country. Here's a complete schedule of the premiere, press conference and the film's worldwide release.

Viral video: Nora Fatehi breaks down after Morocco's World Cup quarter-final exit Actor-performer Nora Fatehi got emotional after Morocco crashed out of the FIFA World Cup in the quarter-finals. A video of the actor wiping away tears in the stands is going viral.

Ali Fazal shares handwritten note to fans for giving Raakh character home in hearts Actor Ali Fazal shared a handwritten note thanking fans for embracing his Raakh character, SI Jayprakash Jatav. The actor said his heart was "full" as the series continues to enjoy a strong global response on Prime Video.

Ajay Devgn's Dhamaal 4 confirms franchise future with Dhamaal 5 tease The makers have announced Dhamaal 5 in the final moments of Ajay Devgn's Dhamaal 4. Filmmaker Farhad Samji will direct the next instalment, even as audience reactions remain mixed.

Prakash Raj gets bail in multiple voter ID case in Bengaluru court Actor Prakash Raj appeared before a Bengaluru court in the voter ID case and sought bail after a non-bailable warrant. The court recalled the warrant after his counsel said summons were never served and granted bail on a cash surety of Rs 4,000.

National award-winning Tamil director-cinematographer Chezhiyan dies at 57 National Award-winning cinematographer, director and writer R Chezhiyan died in Chennai on Friday after a prolonged illness. Colleagues, actors and fans remembered his realist cinema and lasting contribution to Tamil films.

Idhayam Murali review: Atharvaa's love story fails to confess its own feelingsRelease Date:Fri Jul 10, 2026 Idhayam Murali movie review: Atharvaa headlines Idhayam Murali as a man who cannot confess his love. The film lands its biggest laughs through cameos and comedy but struggles to deepen its relationships.

general news.headline: Boat carrying several Indian tourists capsizes near Phu Quoc Island in VietnamThe Indian Embassy said it is coordinating with local authorities after a boat carrying several Indian tourists capsized near Vietnam's Phu Quoc Island, where search and rescue operations remain underway.

The blessing and curse of being Novak Djokovic at 39Novak Djokovic beat time in Melbourne, then outlasted Felix Auger-Aliassime at Wimbledon. Jannik Sinner ended it in straight sets. At 39, one match from a 25th major, he is living the strange arithmetic of a career that refuses to let him rest.

Cops say Siya killed Ketan, proving it will be easier said than donePune Police has alleged that Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary pushed Ketan Agarwal off Lohagad Fort. The case now rests on whether investigators can prove an unbroken chain of circumstantial evidence.

How PM Modi's New Zealand schedule counters viral 'nap break' reportA viral New Zealand media report claimed India sought 'nap breaks' in PM Modi's Auckland itinerary. However, his jam-packed official schedule busted the claims. Read on to find out how.
The Panipat route: How clothes from around the world reach Sarojini"Sarojini ke kapde pehen ke jaati madam disco." But where do those clothes actually come from? We went to Panipat to find out.

Toddler suffers facial injury, dies days later from anaesthesia complicationsThe 18-month-old boy died at a private hospital in Kerala's Kannur district on Friday, July 10. He was his parents' only child and was born nearly eight years after their wedding.
India's warship building worriesA burst of inductions but concerns about a gap in orders and indigenous technology

Video: On way to work, man gets electrocuted and slips into rainwater-filled drainThe man lost his life in the incident, which took place on Thursday, July 9, when Noida and several other parts of the wider NCR region were lashed by incessant rains.

Scaloni fires back over FIFA favouritism claims: Some don't want Argentina to winFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Scaloni rejected claims FIFA is favouring Argentina after the controversial comeback win over Egypt. He said resentment towards the champions is motivating his squad before the quarter-final against Switzerland.

Sabrina Pasterski built a plane at 12, rejected NASA, now called the next EinsteinSabrina Gonzalez Pasterski built and flew an aircraft at the age of 14, graduated from MIT with a perfect GPA in three years, had her research cited by Stephen Hawking, and turned down offers from NASA, Blue Origin and a reported $1.1 million academic position to pursue some of physics' biggest unanswered questions.

Twist in Jaipur woman's murder, daughter now accused of killing father tooAyushi Sharma's maternal uncle has sought an investigation into her father Vijay Sharma's death. The move adds a fresh allegation to the Jaipur case, where police have already arrested seven accused.

8th Pay Commission: Why its impact goes far beyond government salariesEconomists say the 8th Pay Commission will affect far more than central government salaries and pensions. Its recommendations could shape consumer demand, state finances, inflation and fiscal planning.

Virat Kohli's privacy invaded in London? England star recalls bizarre cafe incidentJordan Cox said Virat Kohli was unknowingly photographed during a private London cafe meeting before the former's Test debut earlier this year. It highlighted the former India captain's difficulty living normally despite staying low-profile.

J&K part of India: Indian diplomat flags incorrect map at Bangladesh seminarIndia objected to an incorrect map depicting Jammu and Kashmir during a SAARC seminar in Dhaka, with Indian diplomat Pooja Kumari Jha asserting that the region is an integral and inalienable part of India.

India has an e-waste problem. Delhi has smelled an opportunityAs technology use increases across India, one policy move in the national capital reflects a wider shift in how governments may approach one of the country's fastest-growing waste streams.

Bishnoi aide held in France for drug trafficking, another to be deported from CanadaCanadian police said Lawrence Bishnoi gang associate Garinder Dev has been arrested in France and will face extradition proceedings. The arrest and a separate deportation order in Canada came days after US prosecutors indicted Bishnoi and Goldy Brar.

Are engineering colleges selling 2015 courses at 2026 prices?Handcrafted ByOur Editors Engineering colleges have added AI, Data Science, Cybersecurity and other future-focused courses to their brochures. But as fees climb sharply, students and recruiters are questioning whether the classroom has evolved at the same pace as the price tag.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Before PepsiCo, Indra Nooyi worked as a receptionist to pay for her Yale studiesBefore becoming one of the world's most recognised business leaders, Indra Nooyi was a student balancing classes and overnight shifts. Her Yale journey, filled with financial challenges and long hours, shaped the discipline that later defined her corporate career.

Sri Lankan woman watches mother, 56, receive Master's degree; gets emotionalA Sri Lankan woman watched from Dubai as her 56-year-old mother received her Master's degree. The moment underscored years of sacrifice, resilience and dreams pursued later in life.

Bring Matt Damon home: How The Odyssey keeps Hollywood's $600 million joke aliveThe old internet gag about Hollywood repeatedly rescuing actor Matt Damon finds new life with The Odyssey. This time, Damon's next great homecoming depends on nobody but himself: a quiet reminder that every great journey, on screen or off, is ultimately about finding your way back. And honestly? We'll still be cheering from the shore.

The regulator has sought detailed compliance records and warned of legal action if the response is delayed.

Women beat men in Indian colleges for seventh year straight, AISHE data showsAISHE 2023-24 data shows Indian women now outpace men in higher education enrolment for the seventh straight year running, with female GER at 31.2 against 28.9 for men and enrolment up 42.2 percent since 2014-15, led by heartland states like Bihar, West Bengal and Kerala.

Monsoon to take a break: Dry weather will return to these placesAfter a spell of widespread showers brought much-needed relief, India's weather is set to shift again, putting celebrations on hold. Here's why.

sports.headline: Indian football team to tour New Zealand for two-match friendly series The Indian men's football team will travel to New Zealand for a historic two-match friendly series during the November 2026 FIFA international window, with the fixtures forming part of celebrations marking 100 years of sporting ties between the two nations.

Norway vs England: Haaland's Vikings stand between England and semis FIFA World Cup, quarter-final: Erling Haaland vs Harry Kane. Norway vs England. Two in-form teams, fresh off impressive Round of 16 victories, now collide in Miami with a place in the semifinals on the line.

Scaloni fires back over FIFA favouritism claims: Some don't want Argentina to win FIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Scaloni rejected claims FIFA is favouring Argentina after the controversial comeback win over Egypt. He said resentment towards the champions is motivating his squad before the quarter-final against Switzerland.

Virat Kohli's privacy invaded in London? England star recalls bizarre cafe incident Jordan Cox said Virat Kohli was unknowingly photographed during a private London cafe meeting before the former's Test debut earlier this year. It highlighted the former India captain's difficulty living normally despite staying low-profile.

India in desperate search for answers with No.1 T20I ranking on the line IND vs ENG: India head into the fifth T20I in Southampton under immense pressure amid a disastrous tour of England, with Shreyas Iyer's side still searching for their first win and facing the prospect of losing their No.1 ranking.

England captain Harry Kane confirms golf outing with US president Donald Trump England captain Harry Kane has confirmed he played a round of golf with U.S. President Donald Trump in Florida around 18 months ago, calling it a "surreal" experience and saying the president is a good golfer.

The blessing and curse of being Novak Djokovic at 39 Novak Djokovic beat time in Melbourne, then outlasted Felix Auger-Aliassime at Wimbledon. Jannik Sinner ended it in straight sets. At 39, one match from a 25th major, he is living the strange arithmetic of a career that refuses to let him rest.

Animesh Kujur clocks 10.14s, records fastest 100m by an Indian on foreign soil India's sprint sensation Animesh Kujur continued his rapid rise by clocking a lifetime best of 10.14 seconds to finish second in the men's 100m final at the PUMA Fast Arms Legs 2026 meet, setting the fastest 100m ever run by an Indian outside the country.

Belgium's Courtois backs Lammens to recover from costly World Cup blunder Belgium goalkeeper Thibaut Courtois threw his support behind Senne Lammens after the young substitute's late mistake proved decisive in Belgium's 2-1 defeat to Spain in the FIFA World Cup quarter-final on Friday.

Stokes retirement video: ICC closes anti-corruption enquiry with no action vs ECB The England and Wales Cricket Board (ECB) will face no action from the International Cricket Council (ICC) after the governing body concluded its enquiry into the release of Ben Stokes' retirement video.

Colombia federation condemns death threats to midfielder Campaz after World Cup exit The Colombian Football Federation on Friday condemned death threats against midfielder Jaminton Campaz and his family after the team's World Cup exit, urging authorities to investigate the abuse and identify those behind it.

Czech mate! Karolina Muchova, Linda Noskova dare to dream in historic finalKarolina Muchova and Linda Noskova will meet in the first all-Czech women's singles final in Wimbledon history, guaranteeing a Czech champion at the All England Club.

Harry Kane brushes aside Haaland comparisons, plays down Golden Boot raceHarry Kane refused to be drawn into comparisons with Erling Haaland on the eve of England's FIFA World Cup quarter-final against Norway, insisting his only focus is leading his team to the title rather than winning the Golden Boot.

Lamine Yamal fires warning to France: They should be afraid of usFIFA World Cup 2026: Lamine Yamal said Spain have nothing to fear before their World Cup semi-final against France in Dallas. His remarks revived Spain's recent wins over France and framed the tie as a clash of the tournament's two best teams.

Thibaut Courtois to Senne Lammens: Belgium's tearful change of the guardBelgium goalkeeper Thibaut Courtois has explained why he was substituted during his side's World Cup quarterfinal defeat to Spain, revealing a muscle injury left him unable to kick long balls before Rudi Garcia opted to replace him in the second half.

Merino's super-sub magic sends Spain into second-ever World Cup semi-finalFIFA World Cup 2026: Mikel Merino came off the bench to score late as Spain beat Belgium 2-1 in the World Cup quarter-final. The win sent Spain into only their second World Cup semi-final and set up a meeting with France.

Sinner's new pre-match ritual: Watching FIFA World Cup, hanging out with friendsJannik Sinner said he stayed relaxed with friends and football before beating Novak Djokovic at Wimbledon. The straight-sets win sent him into the final and highlighted the calm behind his sharp form.

Sachin Tendulkar and Shubman Gill steal spotlight in Wimbledon Royal BoxSachin Tendulkar and Shubman Gill watched Wimbledon from the Royal Box as Alexander Zverev beat Arthur Fery to reach the final. The India Test and ODI captain made the most of a short break before returning to lead the side in the ODI series against England.

Sarvesh Kushare's Diamond League debut sees him snatch podium in men's high jumpSarvesh Kushare announced himself on the Diamond League stage with a historic podium finish in Monaco, clearing 2.26m to finish third in the men's high jump. The Indian national record holder became only the fourth Indian athlete to finish in the top three at a Diamond League meet.

Djokovic promises Wimbledon return after semi-final battering vs SinnerNovak Djokovic has vowed to return to Wimbledon next year despite suffering a crushing straight-sets defeat to Jannik Sinner in the semi-finals. The 39-year-old said he wants "at least one more" shot at the All England Club.

Sinner demolishes Djokovic at Wimbledon semis in ruthless revengeJannik Sinner avenged his Australian Open semi-final defeat to Novak Djokovic with a commanding straight-sets win at Wimbledon, beating the Serbian legend 6-4, 6-4, 6-4 to reach his second successive final at the All England Club.

Lord's Test Day 1: India Women stutter to 285, but strike late to keep hosts at bayIndia Women were bowled out for 285 despite fifties from Smriti Mandhana, Harmanpreet Kaur and Deepti Sharma, but struck late through Kranti Goud to leave England at 21 for 1 at stumps on an evenly poised opening day at Lord's.

ESP 2-1 BEL Highlights: Merino's late goal knocks out Belgium in quarter-finalSpain (ESP) vs Belgium (BEL) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (ESP 2-1 BEL) Spain seal a dramatic 2-1 win over Belgium to reach the FIFA World Cup semi-finals. Super-sub Mikel Merino's late strike proves decisive as La Roja survive a nervy finish to book a meeting with France.

Cristiano Ronaldo's future, age and more: What new Portugal coach Jorge Jesus saidNew Portugal coach Jorge Jesus addressed Cristiano Ronaldo's international future during his unveiling, insisting the veteran remains part of his plans despite retiring from World Cup football and revealing he will hold talks before making a decision.

Zverev crushes Arthur Fery's Wimbledon dream to storm into maiden finalFresh off his maiden French Open triumph, Alexander Zverev continued his red-hot form by ending Arthur Fery's dream Wimbledon run to book a first final at the All England Club.

India A set for landmark tour of Nepal for three-match T20I series: ExclusiveIndia A will tour Nepal for a landmark three-match T20I series on December 9, 11 and 13, India Today has learnt. The series marks a major boost for Nepal cricket, offering the hosts a chance to test themselves against India's emerging stars.
FIFA World Cup 2026 | Why India is missingAs smaller nations light up the World Cup, Indian football languishes in neglect and indecision

Highlights: Sinner beats Djokovic in straight sets, sets up final vs ZverevWimbledon semifinals Highlights: Novak Djokovic's run at Wimbledon gets cut short by World No.1 Jannik Sinner. The Italian beats Djokovic in straight sets (6-4,6-4, 6-4), booking a place in the final against Zlexander Zverev, who earlier beat British wildcard Arthur Fery in straight games.

Shree Charani rewarded for T20 World Cup heroics, earns maiden Test cap at Lord'sENGW vs INDW, Only Test Day 1: Shree Charani made her Test debut at the Lord's after her stupendous campaign in the Women's T20 World Cup where she took 14 wickets from five matches.

Only Women's Test, Day 1: HIGHLIGHTSEngland vs India, Only Women's Test, Day 1 Highlights: England have won the toss and opted to field first at the Lord's. Alice Capsey and Mady Villiers are making their Test debuts for the hosts while Shree Charani got her maiden Test cap for India.

England chase No.1 T20I ranking after series win vs India, says Harry BrookEngland captain Harry Brook has targeted the No. 1 spot in the ICC men's T20I rankings after his side wrapped up the series against India, calling it "definitely a prize" worth chasing.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
